emergent_themes.description: Health professionals experiencing tension between their professional knowledge and personal convenience behaviors
Users actively managing their own behavioral triggers by cancelling subscriptions to control ordering frequency
Food quality standards declining over time due to increased time pressure and life demands
Intentional ordering with next-day meal planning in mind as efficiency strategy

coding_summary: P017 represents a unique case of a health professional (dietitian) who relies heavily on food delivery despite professional knowledge. Key findings include: (1) Time pressure from work+study schedule is the primary delivery trigger, (2) She demonstrates sophisticated self-regulation by cancelling Deliveroo Plus to control ordering frequency, (3) Her food standards have deteriorated over time from healthy options to 'whatever', (4) She strategically orders with leftovers in mind for next-day meals, (5) Professional identity creates internal conflict but doesn't override convenience needs, (6) Social influence from friends drove initial subscription adoption, (7) She shows awareness gaps about student benefits despite being a postgrad student.

coverage_gaps.reason: P017 lives alone and orders solo frequently - no stigma expressed, appears normalized for her lifestyle
